page: Microsoft Azure E64as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EASv4 server family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The configuration delivers 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes 885 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the E64as_v4 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and memory performance. The Passmark memory score ranks in the top 10% tier, supported by a cached read-only small block memory bandwidth of 4162.87 GB/sec. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both place in the top 25%. However, uncached large-block memory bandwidth drops to average levels, with read speeds at 107.94 GB/sec and write speeds at 51.36 GB/sec. Single-core performance, such as Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression, also falls within the average range.

The server exhibits strong database and transactional performance, as evidenced by a Redis SET throughput of 62.62 million operations per second and high Passmark database operations scores. For machine learning workloads, the hardware handles prompt processing and text generation efficiently on small 135M models, but text generation on large 70B models is weak, averaging only 2.76 tokens per second due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation make it a viable option for memory-intensive enterprise applications. This server is best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems, software compilation, and data-dense enterprise workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure E64as_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 3.525 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 32 physical cores, 885 GB of local storage, and is premium storage capable. Benchmark results show top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core processing, though uncached memory bandwidth and single-core operations remain average. The server excels in database operations and memory-intensive tasks, achieving 62.62 million Redis SET operations per second and strong multi-threaded decompression. However, without GPU acceleration, it exhibits weak performance for large-scale LLM text generation, averaging 2.76 tokens per second on 70B models. It is highly suited for large databases, in-memory caching, and software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64as_v4 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It excels in database operations and multi-core workloads but lacks GPU acceleration.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64as_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, ideal for databases and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and substantial local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores.
Equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM and 885 GB of local storage.
Top-tier memory performance with strong multi-core benchmark results.
Delivers high database throughput, achieving 62.62 million Redis SET operations per second.
Best suited for large relational databases, in-memory caching, and software compilation.
